programme.title: David Jeremiah
programme.desc: In this weekly program, Dr. David Jeremiah delivers practical messages to the congregation at the Shadow Mountain Community Church in San Diego. Also featured is music from the Shadow Mountain Community Church Choir and Orchestra, as well as performances by musical guests that have included Steve Green and Charles Billingsley. Men and women who have been impacted by the Turning Point ministry also share their stories with viewers. During the month of December, "Turning Point" usually features special Christmas programming.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Unexplained Edition

And might the enigmatic Peralta Stones reveal the path to desert gold? Tony Harris and his team investigate compelling footage of ancient ruins and relics from around the world and deliver their verdict.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Unexplained Edition
programme.desc: What's behind the brain-numbing noise tormenting Brooklyn residents?; has Bigfoot's call finally been caught on tape?; are the eerie sounds recorded on Mars a message from an alien race?; Tony Harris and his team investigate chilling audio evidence.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: It is hard to believe that the beginnings of modern endoscopy are really all thanks to one professional sword-swallower; or that lack of sleep has some strange upsides; these are some unthinkable experiments.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: One-of-a-kind crimes are often met with one-of-a-kind punishments; like a pig whose bloody rampage lands itself in the courtroom, or a music enthusiast whose wall-rattling beats subject them to hours of classical symphonies.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: We've heard the tales of successful prison breaks, but these aren't your average escapes; from a man who frees himself all thanks to a stone fruit, to a Soviet pilot that evades capture by stealing the enemy's plane.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Human bones as a "miracle cure" for gout; living mummies buried alive; a truly bizarre Victorian way to lose weight -- with tapeworms; from daily rites to once-in-a-lifetime rituals, the stranger side of human nature is revealed in these rituals.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: From a dog's space voyage, to a harrowing story of Arctic survival, to various trips through time; journeys so extraordinary that they are truly unbelievable.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Accidents happen all the time, but every so often, a few defy logic and leave the world stunned; from a man blasted by a high-pressure air hose, to a lumberjack who survived near decapitation, they border on the impossible.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: From riding massive tree trunks down a treacherous slope, to hosting a barrage of monkeys for an all-you-can-eat buffet, to a food fight of epic proportions; these are the stories of history's most bizarre and extraordinary traditions.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: From calling Veteran's Stadium home, to setting up camp in an airport terminal, to turning an unassuming dumpster into a luxurious abode, each of these dwellings are so beyond odd, that they are truly unbelievable.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: From a piece of the International Space Station crashing into a Florida home, to the first chimpanzee blasted into space, to a mind-bending timeslip experienced by two women in Versailles--this episode dives into the unbelievable stories that challenge what we think we know about our place in the universe.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: When the sun sets, strange things come alive. Imagine a full-scale war waged against thousands of invading frogs, an artist who only paints while sleepwalking, and battlefield wounds that literally glow in the dark. These bizarre and unbelievable moments all have one thing in common--they happen only when darkness falls.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.desc: Could a pulsating light seen over Cuba be a UFO? A creature is seen swimming in a New York lake. Is there an American "Loch Ness" Monster? Have gelatinous alien eggs been found underwater by divers? A hospital wheelchair moves on its own. Is it possessed by a ghost? Tony Harris and his team of experts examine the evidence to find the answers.

programme.desc: AI software identifies lameness early in dairy cows; the rewarding work of controlling invasive plant and fish pests in Te Arawa Lakes; and the feasibility of growing almonds in Central Hawkes Bay.

programme.title: Hyundai Country Calendar
programme.desc: An ex-lawyer creates a community of food producers to provide an affordable and resilient local food supply, to mend what she believes is a broken food system.

programme.title: Susan Calman's Great British Cities
programme.desc: Susan Calman unearths the historical stories of great conurbations such as Liverpool, Bath, Cambridge, Leeds, Newcastle and Glasgow, revealing their secret stories and the people behind their past.

programme.desc: Couples looking to relocate from the city find their perfect country house in a regional, Canadian location.
